---
title: "Protein Motif Analysis"
subtitle: "Eukaryotic Linear Motifs in Potyviridae CI helicase — ELM database search and annotation"
author: "chrissi m."
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
  error: true
---


## Protein Motif Analysis using ELM 

```{r setup}
#| include: false

# Load required libraries
library(Biostrings)
library(tidyverse)
library(DECIPHER) # used mainly for removing gaps here. which is way below the package capabilities...

source(here::here("R", "elm_motif_search.R"))
source(here::here("R", "elm_motif_search_API.R"))
```

```{r}
protaln <- Biostrings::readAAStringSet("https://ictv.global/sites/default/files/report_files/OPSR.Poty.Fig3.v16_align.txt", format = "fasta")
```

```{r}

cons <- consensusString(protaln, ambiguityMap = "X")
anchora <- start(matchPattern("VASYN", cons, max.mismatch = 1))[1]
anchorb <- end(matchPattern("VASYN", cons, max.mismatch = 1))[1]

aln <- as(protaln, "AAStringSet")
aa <- DECIPHER::RemoveGaps(aln)
aa
region <- subseq(aln, anchora - 20, anchorb + 20)

region
```

The sequence names indicate the virus names (abbreviated) and the sequence accessions. To sort properly by the different genera, I will create a table from the names and merge that with isolate metadata that is also available via ICTV.


```{r}
#| warning: false
library(readxl)

# read file
ictv <- readxl::read_xlsx(
  here::here("data", "VMR_MSL41.v1.20260320.xlsx"), sheet = 2) %>% 
  # cleaning: keep only Family members of Potyviridae and select non empty columns only
  dplyr::filter(Family == "Potyviridae") %>% 
  dplyr::select(where(~ any(!is.na(.))))

ictv
```

```{r}
nms <- names(region)
nms

tb <- tibble(nms) %>% dplyr::mutate(virus_abbr = gsub("\\_.*", "", nms), accession = gsub(".*\\_", "", nms))
tb
```


```{r}
library(stringr)
library(fuzzyjoin)


result <- fuzzyjoin::fuzzy_left_join(
  tb, ictv,
  by = c("accession" = "Virus GENBANK accession"), 
  match_fun = ~ stringr::str_detect(.y, regex(.x, ignore_case = TRUE)))

result

```


```{r}
conc <- build_concat(as.character(region))


## workaround for upcoming mess : 
# Biostrings::writeXStringSet(region, here::here("notebooks", "data", "ELM_query.fasta"))
seq <- Biostrings::AAStringSet(conc$concat)
# needed later
jmap <- conc$map

# Biostrings::writeXStringSet(seq, here::here("data", "ELM_query_cat.fasta"))

# ce <- check_elm()
# elms <- query_elm(sequence = conc$concat)
```

The thing with ELM is, I could submit each sequence via the API, but would have to limit to 1 request per minute. Makes 3 hours...Guess I will let that run in the background, as well. But I also run ELM on the narrower region around the motif: 

http://elm.eu.org/start_search/

```{r}

```

# 
comment: need to integrate gget here. Did not work, takes too much time. Did it manually. 

The sequence was queried in the eukaryotic linear motif database (http://elm.eu.org/; https://academic.oup.com/nar/article/52/D1/D442/7420098). 

Screenshots of the output are provided in images/. 

![Linear Motifs in concatenated polypeptide sequences of homologous CI fragments from different species of Potyviridae](../images/ELM_graphics.png)
The results table was exported and is included in ../data/ELM_table.txt

[Linear Motifs in concatenated polypeptide sequences of homologous CI fragments from different species of Potyviridae](http://elm.eu.org/data/QiKNoNehtR/QiKNoNehtR.png)




```{r}
# ELM_results.txt is the manually-exported table from http://elm.eu.org/
# To automate: potytools::elm_batch_search("data/ELM_query_cat.fasta", output_dir = "data/")
elm_path <- here::here("data", "ELM_results.txt")
if (!file.exists(elm_path)) {
  message("ELM_results.txt not found. Run potytools::elm_batch_search() to generate it.")
  knitr::knit_exit()
}
elm <- readr::read_delim(elm_path, delim = "\t")
colnames(elm) <- gsub(" ", "_", colnames(elm))
colnames(elm) <- gsub("-", "_", colnames(elm))
head(elm)

elm <- fill(elm, c(Elm_Name, Elm_Description, Pattern, PHI_Blast_Instance_Mapping, Structural_Filter_Info, Probability), .by = NULL, .direction = "down") %>% dplyr::rename_with(tolower)

head(elm) 

elm <- elm %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(elm_name))
elm
```
From here I can re-use some code that claude generated for me earlier. Then I noticed, there's a python package to do the same. I got it to work, but to run it locally, I need their database and my notebook is running extremely low on storage. 

So, I did it manually, but can go from the table above. If the claude-code would have retrieved the hits, the output of the function would have been a table with headers: Data frame with columns:
#'   elm_identifier, elm_name, start, end, sequence_match,
#'   probability, logic, phiblast_match (where available). 

Already close to the copy-pasted stuff. Just need to split the coordinates - good idea to do that anyways: 

```{r}

# split Positions at "-"
elm <- elm %>% separate(positions, into = c("start", "end"), sep = "-")
# remove aa indicator "[A]" remaining in "end"
elm$end <- gsub(" \\[A\\]", "", elm$end)
# convert start and end to integer
elm$start <- as.integer(elm$start)
elm$end <- as.integer(elm$end)

# cell compartment has a bit of an odd format. I will group all elm motifs first to then correct the formatting by pasting all unique entries. This will add an " NA" at the end of the adjusted column. This is removed afterwards.
elm <- elm %>% group_by(elm_name) %>% 
  # create new column (note the original has an additional "_" at the end)
  mutate(cell_compartment = paste(unique(cell_compartment_), collapse = " "))
# remove the introduced NA at the end
elm$cell_compartment <- gsub(" NA$", "", elm$cell_compartment)
# remove obsolete column
elm <- elm %>% select(-cell_compartment_) %>% ungroup()
elm
```

```{r}
elm_result <- elm
elmflag <- flag_junction_hits(elm, jmap)
elmflag
```

While searching for a domain that I could retrieve systematically, I stumbled over one in which the motif is present. I checked where else I could find it... 

https://rest.uniprot.org/uniprotkb/search?compressed=true&format=fasta&query=%28%28reviewed%3Atrue%29+AND+%28xref%3Aprosite-PS51194%29+AND+%28taxonomy_name%3A%22Viruses%22%29%29&size=500

```{r}

```

-------------------------------------------
##################################################

```{r}
elmapi <- elm_query_concatenated(conc)
```
